sections.paragraph:
an unused root.-

(1) prop. to cut, to cut up, to separate; cogn. to בָּדַד No. I., which see; (Arab. فَذَّ V. X., to be alone, separated; in the Indo-germanic languages, Sanscr. pat, to cleave; Lat. pando, findo).

(2) specially to plow; whence فَدَّادُ a plough-ox; and فَدَّانُ a yoke of oxen, plough; Syr. and Chald, ܦܰܕܳܢܐܳ, פַּדַּן id.; ܦܝܺܕܢܐܳ field, plain. [This is called in Thes. “an uncertain root”].

Derivative פַּדָּן.
